title: Reconcile Grabowski worktrees and transient units
goal: Classify Grabowski worktrees and failed transient units against live tasks, PRs and receipts, then archive or clean only terminal residue through bounded lifecycle paths.

acceptance.assertion: Every candidate worktree and transient unit is classified as active, blocked, terminal-retain, terminal-archive or safe-cleanup using live Git, PR, task and systemd evidence.
No active branch, dirty worktree, running task or unresolved receipt is removed.
Cleanup uses existing lifecycle/reconcile grips or a new narrow fail-closed path, never broad filesystem deletion.
Worktree and failed-unit counts plus Grabowski health are rechecked after effects.

metadata.blocking_condition: Sixteen newly archived clean terminal checkouts remain inside the mandatory 24-hour cleanup grace. The complete batch becomes cleanup-eligible no earlier than 2026-07-21T04:24:53Z (2026-07-21 06:24:53 Europe/Berlin), subject to a fresh dry run and unchanged live coordination evidence. Additional dirty, active, retained or manager-owned checkouts still require individual classification.

classification_basis: zero live resource leases at archive time
durable recovery refs created before any future removal
does_not_establish: physical removal of the sixteen newly archived checkouts
classification completion for every remaining dirty, active, retained or manager-owned checkout
runtime_correctness beyond the recorded live readbacks
cleanup permission before mandatory grace elapses
